title: Database connection pool exhausted causing request queuing and latency spike

task.summary: Detect, localize, and repair a connection pool exhaustion incident causing elevated p99 latency and request timeouts.
task.instructions: Use the incident evidence to confirm the pool is exhausted, localize it to a long-running transaction holding connections, and submit a repair conclusion through the ops workflow.
task.acceptance_notes: This local ops task is synthetic but uses realistic incident framing.

visible_context.issue_text: p99 API latency has spiked from 120ms to over 8s. Some requests are timing out entirely. Database CPU and query times look normal. The connection pool wait queue is growing.
error_logs: api-server: connection pool wait timeout after 5000ms (pool_size=20, checked_out=20, waiting=47)
pg: slow query log — transaction open for 312s on connection id=14 (query: BEGIN; SELECT ... FOR UPDATE)
reproduction_steps: Inspect the pool checkout metrics and identify connections held longer than expected.
Locate the long-running transaction holding a pool slot.
Decide whether the fault has been detected, localized, and repaired.
visible_context.notes: The expected repair terminates the runaway transaction and adds a statement timeout to prevent future unbounded transactions.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic incident where a long-running uncommitted transaction exhausts the DB connection pool.

notes: Synthetic local DB connection pool exhaustion ops task.
